thread.title: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'
thread.title_full: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'

author: JACKSON WALKER | The National News Desk
title: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'
text: {p}Former President Bill Clinton on Thursday said he was flattered to be described as the country’s first Black president, adding that he viewed the title as a compliment.{/p}{p}The late Toni Morrison bestowed the title on Clinton in a 1998 piece for {em}The New Yorker{/em}. She claimed Clinton embodied several characteristics of Black people.{/p}{p}“After all, Clinton displays almost every trope of blackness: single-parent household, born poor, working-class, saxophone-playing, McDonald’s-and-junk-food-loving boy from Arkansas,” Morrison wrote. “And when virtually all the African-American Clinton appointees began, one by one, to disappear, when the President’s body, his privacy, his unpoliced sexuality became the focus of the persecution, when he was metaphorically seized and body-searched, who could gainsay these black men who knew whereof they spoke?”{/p}{p}Clinton revisited the remark during a book promotion event, saying he was honored by it.{/p}{p}“It was halfway serious and halfway making fun of me,” Clinton said of Morrison’s characterization of him. “I took that as a great compliment.”{/p}{p}“It wasn’t that I owe it to my ancestors because I grew up in a white, Southern, working-class family where my grandfather owned a country store and all of his customers were Black, and he loved them and they loved him,” he added.{/p}{p}Clinton continued by describing how his grandfather would often allow Black customers to take food without paying if they were unable to provide for themselves, as the country had not yet created the federal food stamp system.{/p}{p}Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., in 2016 jokingly described former President Barack Obama as America’s second Black president in reference to Morrison’s comment.{/p}{p}“First black president right?” Sen. Sanders asked {em}The Hollywood Reporter{/em}. “Obama is only the second black president.”{/p}{p}“This president will go down in history as one of the smartest presidents we had in this country,” Sen. Sanders added of Obama. “I think people are beginning to catch on. The more they hear from Republicans, the smarter they think he is.”{/p}{p}Morrison told {em}Time Magazine{/em} in 2008 readers misunderstood her decision to describe Clinton as she had.{/p}{p}“People misunderstood that phrase,” she said. “I was deploring the way in which President Clinton was being treated, vis-à-vis the sex scandal that was surrounding him. I said he was being treated like a black on the street, already guilty, already a perp. I have no idea what his real instincts are, in terms of race.”{/p}{p}{em}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\"}Follow {/a}Jackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. Have a news tip? Send it to jacwalker@sbgtv.com.{/em}{/p}{p}FRIDAY: Mostly sunny, with highs in the mid 50s. Winds are light and out of the northeast.{/p}{p}SATURDAY: Partly cloudy and warmer. Highs will be in the mid 60s, with a southeasterly wind at 10-15 mph.{/p}{p}Gusty winds return Sunday, boosting temperatures to the low to mid 70s. Another dry cold front arrives Monday, dropping our highs to the mid 50s. We're still watching a system that is set to arrive by the middle of the holiday week. Rain chances will return to the forecast sometime near and around Thanksgiving, but nothing terribly impactful looks likely at this time. We will keep watching.{/p}{p}Available {a href=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\"}HERE{/a} for iOS
